title: Create /auth/callback page and token exchange handler
description: Create auth-callback.html (served at /auth/callback). This page handles the URL fragment or query params from Supabase's email redirect, exchanges the tokens, and hands off to the routing logic in TASK-018.
acceptance_criteria: On load, it calls supabase.auth.exchangeCodeForSession() (PKCE flow) or reads the fragment (implicit flow) — determined by Supabase client version
On success, the user's session is established in localStorage
On error (expired link, invalid token), page shows a friendly error and a link to /signup
Page shows a loading spinner while the exchange is in progress

steps.details: Minimal page: shared card container, a spinner div, and an error div (hidden). Title: 'Signing you in…'

Supabase JS v2 handles the fragment/code automatically when the client is initialised. Listen for the auth state change: supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange((event, session) => { if (event === 'SIGNED_IN' && session) { handlePostAuth(session); } else if (event === 'TOKEN_REFRESHED') { handlePostAuth(session); } });
Also call: const { error } = await supabase.auth.getSession(); if (error || !session) { showError('This link has expired. Please sign up again.'); }

technical_notes: Supabase JS v2 automatically parses the URL hash/query on client init and fires onAuthStateChange. The /auth/callback page primarily needs to listen for that event rather than manually parse the URL.
